name: ccf

metadata.name: ccf
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Apache Software License
metadata.summary: Set of tools and utilities for the Confidential Consortium Framework (CCF)

documentation:
# CCF Python

Suite of Python tools for the Confidential Consortium Framework (CCF).

For more information, please find the CCF documentation at https://microsoft.github.io/CCF.

Package sources are available at https://github.com/microsoft/CCF/tree/main/python.

functions.name: ccf.receipt.root
documentation:
Recompute root of Merkle tree from a leaf and a proof of the form:
[{"left": digest}, {"right": digest}, ...]

functions.name: ccf.receipt.verify
documentation: Verify signature over root of Merkle Tree

functions.name: ccf.receipt.check_endorsement
documentation: Check endorser has endorsed endorsee

functions.name: ccf.receipt.check_endorsements
documentation: Check a node certificate is endorsed by a service certificate, transitively through a list of endorsements.

classes.name: ccf.ledger.PublicDomain

methods.name: ccf.ledger.PublicDomain.get_tables
documentation:
Return a dictionary of all public tables (with their content) in a {py:class}`ccf.ledger.Transaction`.

```
return

Dictionary of public tables with their content.
```

methods.name: ccf.ledger.PublicDomain.get_seqno
documentation: Return the sequence number at which the transaction was recorded in the ledger.

methods.name: ccf.ledger.PublicDomain.get_claims_digest
documentation: Return the claims digest when there is one

methods.name: ccf.ledger.PublicDomain.get_commit_evidence_digest
documentation: Return the commit evidence digest when there is one
All public tables within a {py:class}`ccf.ledger.Transaction`.

classes.name: ccf.ledger.TxBundleInfo
documentation: Bundle for transaction information required for validation

classes.name: ccf.ledger.LedgerValidator

methods.name: ccf.ledger.LedgerValidator.add_transaction
documentation:
To validate the ledger, ledger transactions need to be added via this method.
Depending on the tables that were part of the transaction, it does different things.
When transaction contains signature table, it starts the verification process and verifies that the root of merkle tree was signed by a node which was part of the network.
It also matches the root of the merkle tree that this class maintains with the one extracted from the ledger.
Further, it validates all service status transitions.
If any of the above checks fail, this method throws.

methods.name: ccf.ledger.LedgerValidator._verify_tx_set
documentation: Verify items 1, 2, and 3 for all the transactions up until a signature.

methods.name: ccf.ledger.LedgerValidator._verify_node_status
documentation: Verify item 1, The merkle root is signed by a valid node in the given network

methods.name: ccf.ledger.LedgerValidator._verify_root_signature
documentation: Verify item 2, that the Merkle root signature validates against the node certificate

methods.name: ccf.ledger.LedgerValidator._verify_merkle_root
documentation: Verify item 3, by comparing the roots from the merkle tree that's maintained by this class and from the one extracted from the ledger

Ledger Validator contains the logic to verify that the ledger hasn't been tampered with.
It has the ability to take transactions and it maintains a MerkleTree data structure similar to CCF.

Ledger is valid and hasn't been tampered with if following conditions are met:
    1) The merkle proof is signed by a Trusted node in the given network
    2) The merkle root and signature are verified with the node cert
    3) The merkle proof is correct for each set of transactions

methods.name: ccf.ledger.Entry.get_public_domain
documentation:
Retrieve the public (i.e. non-encrypted) domain for that entry.

Note: Even if the entry is private-only, an empty {py:class}`ccf.ledger.PublicDomain` object is returned.

```
return

```

methods.name: ccf.ledger.Entry.get_private_domain_size
documentation: Retrieve the size of the private (i.e. encrypted) domain for that transaction.

classes.name: ccf.ledger.Transaction

methods.name: ccf.ledger.Transaction.get_raw_tx
documentation:
Return raw transaction bytes.

```
return

Raw transaction bytes.
```
A transaction represents one entry in the CCF ledger.

classes.name: ccf.ledger.Snapshot
documentation: Utility used to parse the content of a snapshot file.

classes.name: ccf.ledger.LedgerChunk
documentation:
Class used to parse and iterate over {py:class}`ccf.ledger.Transaction` in a CCF ledger chunk.

```
param str name

Name for a single ledger chunk.

param LedgerValidator ledger_validator

 instance used to verify ledger integrity.
```

classes.name: ccf.ledger.Ledger

methods.name: ccf.ledger.Ledger.get_transaction
documentation:
Return the {py:class}`ccf.ledger.Transaction` recorded in the ledger at the given sequence number.

Note that the transaction returned may not yet be verified by a
signature transaction nor committed by the service.

```
param int seqno

Sequence number of the transaction to fetch.

return

```

methods.name: ccf.ledger.Ledger.get_latest_public_state
documentation:
Return the current public state of the service.

Note that the public state returned may not yet be verified by a
signature transaction nor committed by the service.

```
return

Tuple[Dict, int]: Tuple containing a dictionary of public tables and their values and the seqno of the state read from the ledger.
```

Class used to iterate over all {py:class}`ccf.ledger.LedgerChunk` stored in a CCF ledger folder.

```
param str name

Ledger directory for a single CCF node.
```

classes.name: ccf.ledger.InvalidRootException
documentation: MerkleTree root doesn't match with the root reported in the signature's table

classes.name: ccf.ledger.InvalidRootSignatureException
documentation: Signature of the MerkleRoot doesn't match with the signature that's reported in the signature's table

classes.name: ccf.ledger.CommitIdRangeException
documentation: Missing ledger chunk in the ledger directory

classes.name: ccf.ledger.UntrustedNodeException
documentation: The signing node wasn't part of the network

classes.name: ccf.ledger.UnknownTransaction
documentation: The transaction at seqno does not exist in ledger
